question_title: Will SYY's market close price on 2026-03-13 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-02?
summary: SYY (Sysco) needs to close higher on 2026-03-13 than its close on 2026-03-02, a 9-trading-day window. The stock is near its 52-week high ($91.16 vs $91.85) after a strong 27% rally from December lows, supported by a Q2 FY2026 earnings beat in January. Monte Carlo simulations using post-earnings volatility (17% annualized) give 50% with zero drift and 54% with market-average drift. The mild positive momentum is partially offset by resistance near the 52-week high and a slightly weak S&P 500. No major catalysts (earnings) fall within the window. Net assessment is modestly above the ~52% unconditional base rate.

factors.description: Unconditional base rate for stock up over 9 trading days is ~52%
Positive recent momentum: +8.4% in 20 days post-earnings, steady uptrend, breakout to new high on 2/27
Post-earnings fundamental support: Q2 FY2026 beat estimates, guidance raised to upper end
Stock near 52-week high (0.75% away) after 27% rally from Dec lows - technical resistance and mean reversion risk
Broad market slightly weak: S&P 500 -1.3% over 20 days, mild headwind

reasoning:
**Forecast: 52.5%** that SYY closes higher on 2026-03-13 than on 2026-03-02.

Key reasoning:
- **Base rate**: Stocks are up over 9 trading days ~52% of the time
- **Momentum (+)**: Strong recent uptrend with +8.4% in 20 days and a breakout to new highs on Feb 27
- **Earnings (+)**: Q2 FY2026 beat with raised guidance; no earnings in the forecast window
- **Resistance (-)**: Stock is only 0.75% from its 52-week high after a 27% rally — mean reversion risk is elevated
- **Market headwind (-)**: S&P 500 has been slightly weak (-1.3% over 20 days)

The net effect is a modest tilt above the unconditional base rate, resulting in a 52.5% probability.
